assessments.calculatorPolicy: Calculator and dictionary are not permitted.
assessments.name: Engineering and Science Admissions Test (ESAT)
assessments.registrationMethod: Create a UAT-UK account, choose the course-required modules, then book a Pearson Test Centre appointment.
assessments.registrationWindow: 2027-entry cycle: October 2026 or January 2027 sitting, subject to university-specific restrictions.
assessments.scoringMethod: A separate 1-9 score per module to one decimal place; no universal pass/fail score.
assessments.testFormat: Five possible computer-based modules; each module has 27 multiple-choice questions and lasts 40 minutes. Most courses require Mathematics 1 plus two further modules.
uncertainties: Required modules and sitting restrictions are course-specific and are taken from university pages in the link records.

assessments.name: Sixth Term Examination Paper in Mathematics (STEP)
assessments.testFormat: Two examinations are available: STEP 2 and STEP 3. No 2027 operational dates are asserted from the reviewed provider page.
uncertainties: Course- and College-specific conditions vary; no universal threshold or 2027 date is asserted.

assessments.calculatorPolicy: Calculator and dictionary are not permitted.
assessments.name: Test of Academic Reasoning for Admissions (TARA)
assessments.registrationMethod: Create a UAT-UK account, then book a Pearson Test Centre appointment.
assessments.registrationWindow: 2027-entry cycle: October 2026 or January 2027 sitting, subject to university-specific restrictions.
assessments.scoringMethod: Critical Thinking and Problem Solving each receive a 1-9 score; the Writing Task is sent to universities and is not scored by UAT-UK.
assessments.testFormat: Three compulsory 40-minute modules: Critical Thinking, Problem Solving and a Writing Task.
uncertainties: Course-specific requirement levels are taken from university sources; no universal threshold is asserted.

assessments.calculatorPolicy: Calculator and dictionary are not permitted.
assessments.name: Test of Mathematics for University Admission (TMUA)
assessments.registrationMethod: Create a UAT-UK account, then book a Pearson Test Centre appointment.
assessments.registrationWindow: 2027-entry cycle: October 2026 or January 2027 sitting, subject to university-specific restrictions.
assessments.scoringMethod: One overall score on a 1-9 scale to one decimal place; no universal pass/fail score.
assessments.testFormat: Two computer-based multiple-choice papers: Applications of Mathematical Knowledge and Mathematical Reasoning; 20 questions and 75 minutes per paper.
uncertainties: Course-specific requirement levels and any score use must be read from the relevant university source; no universal threshold is asserted.

conditions: Applicants unable to take TMUA after its booking deadline may receive a conditional offer requiring at least one STEP paper.
Applicants applying on or before the last TMUA booking deadline are required to take TMUA; Imperial expects most offers to go to TMUA takers.
Applicants for 2027 entry must sit ESAT for selection.
All applicants must take TMUA; no fixed score threshold is published.
All applicants for admission in 2027/28 must sit TARA alongside the UCAS application.
STEP achievement may be taken into account as part of an alternative offer; it is not a universal application threshold.
All applicants must take Mathematics 1, Mathematics 2 and Physics modules.
Peterhouse applicants may be asked for grade 2 in STEP II.
Cambridge Mathematics offers usually include STEP conditions with College variation.
All applicants must take TMUA at an authorised assessment centre.
Not factored into offer-making; may be considered after A-level results are released.
Not factored into offer-making; may be considered after A-level results are released.
All applicants for Oxford Computer Science courses must take TMUA.
The current 2027 official Oxford course page lists TMUA; the earlier MAT statement is not carried forward.
Grade 3 or above receives additional consideration but does not replace a third A level.
If taken and grade 2 is achieved, the official page gives an alternative AAB offer; STEP is not stated as compulsory.
Grade 2 in STEP is an offer condition for applicants who have not taken TMUA, except contextual-offer applicants.
Typically required for all applicants except those eligible for a contextual offer.
